registration.title: Smart Registration Guide
registration.subtitle: Your TL;DR guide to getting registered on the Electoral Roll. Demystifying forms and documents.
registration.form6_desc: Use this to get added to the Electoral Roll and get your new Voter ID (EPIC card).
registration.form8_desc: Use this if you need to correct details like spelling, address, or if you shifted to a new city.
registration.form7_desc: Use this to delete a deceased person's name or remove a duplicate entry from the list.
registration.photo_desc: Recent color photo with white background (max 2MB)
registration.age_proof_desc: Birth Certificate, 10th/12th Marksheet, PAN, or Aadhaar
registration.address_proof_desc: Water/Electricity Bill, Aadhaar, Passport, or Rent Agreement
registration.death_cert_desc: Required to delete a deceased person from the roll
registration.proof_change_desc: Document supporting the correction (e.g. new address proof, corrected spelling proof)
registration.pro_tip_desc: Ensure uploads are clear. Files are kept local in this prototype and won't be sent to any server.
registration.register_desc: Registration is free, fully online, and takes only 10 minutes on the official Election Commission of India portal.

results.title: Counting & Results
results.subtitle: Demystifying how votes are counted and how the winner is declared in Indian elections.
results.step1_desc: Votes recorded in the Control Unit of EVMs are counted electronically in the presence of candidates' agents.
results.step2_desc: Mandatory verification of VVPAT slips from 5 randomly selected polling stations per assembly constituency is done.
results.step3_desc: The Returning Officer declares the result after completing the counting and VVPAT matching.
results.fptp_desc: India uses the First-Past-The-Post (FPTP) electoral system. This means the candidate with the highest number of votes wins, even if they don't secure an absolute majority (more than 50%).
results.winner_desc: Candidate A wins the seat, despite 60% of voters choosing someone else.
results.live_results_note: Live counting data is usually published on results.eci.gov.in on counting day.

factcheck.title: Fact-Check Desk
factcheck.subtitle: Verify rumors and expose fake news using the Election Commission's official 'Myth vs Reality' logic.
factcheck.report_desc: Did you receive a suspicious WhatsApp forward about the elections? Search our database or submit it for verification.
factcheck.myth1_q: EVMs can be hacked remotely via Bluetooth or WiFi.
factcheck.myth1_a: EVMs are standalone machines. They have no radio frequency receiver, internet connection, or wireless chips.
factcheck.myth2_q: If I press a button, the vote automatically goes to the ruling party.
factcheck.myth2_a: The VVPAT system provides a physical paper trail. You can verify your vote through the glass window for 7 seconds.
factcheck.myth3_q: My name was deleted from the voter list because I voted against the local MLA last time.
factcheck.myth3_a: Voting is 100% secret. Nobody can know who you voted for, so targeted deletion based on voting preference is impossible.
factcheck.submit_failed: Failed to submit report. Please try again.
factcheck.connect_error: Error connecting to server. Please check your connection.

polling.title: Polling Day: Step-by-Step
polling.subtitle: Nervous about your first time voting? Here is exactly what happens from the moment you leave your house.
polling.step1_desc: Find your booth on NVSP portal before leaving home.
polling.step2_desc: Voter ID, Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, or any of the 12 approved IDs.
polling.step3_desc: Enter the polling station and stand in the queue.
polling.step4_desc: Verifies your name in the electoral roll and checks your ID proof.
polling.step5_desc: Marks your finger with indelible ink and gives you a signed slip.
polling.step6_desc: Checks the ink mark and takes your slip before allowing you to vote.
polling.step7_desc: Go to the voting compartment. Press the blue button next to your candidate.
polling.step8_desc: Look at the VVPAT window. The paper slip shows your vote for 7 seconds.
polling.step9_desc: A long beep confirms your vote has been officially recorded.
polling.step10_desc: Exit the polling station immediately after casting your vote.

rights.title: Know Your Rights
rights.subtitle: Most voters are unaware of their legal rights at the polling booth. Familiarize yourself and don't let anyone scam or force you.
rights.right1_desc: Nobody can force you to reveal who you voted for. Your vote is completely confidential.
rights.right2_desc: If you do not like any of the candidates, you have the right to press the NOTA button at the end of the EVM.
rights.right3_desc: If your name is on the electoral roll, you cannot be denied the right to vote, even if your name has a minor spelling error.
rights.right4_desc: Disabled or elderly voters have the right to bring a companion to the voting booth to help them cast their vote.
rights.right5_desc: If someone offers you money or liquor for your vote, or if you spot any violations, you have the right to report it via the cVIGIL app.
rights.warning_desc: If anyone tries to bribe you or force you to vote for a specific candidate, consider them an unfit candidate trying to scam the democratic process.

evm.title: Virtual Voting Unit (EVM)
evm.subtitle: Practice voting in a safe environment. Click the blue button next to your candidate.
evm.vvpat_desc: The slip is visible for 7 seconds before falling into the box.
evm.ready_msg: Please cast your vote by pressing the blue button.
evm.recorded_msg: Vote recorded. Please verify your VVPAT slip (Visible for 7s).
evm.complete_msg: Slip cut and dropped into the sealed box. Voting process complete.

candidate.title: Candidate Intel
candidate.subtitle: Don't vote blindly based on caste, religion, or money. Review affidavits filed on MyNeta.info and track actual promises delivered versus pending.

complaints.title: Legal Complaint Generator
complaints.subtitle: Report Model Code of Conduct (MCC) violations instantly. Use our legally-vetted templates to generate a formal complaint letter ready for submission.
complaints.mcc_title: What is the Model Code of Conduct?
complaints.mcc_desc: The MCC is a set of guidelines issued by the Election Commission of India. It comes into force the moment election dates are announced and stays until results are declared.
complaints.mcc_rule1: No new government schemes can be announced.
complaints.mcc_rule3: No bribery, cash, or liquor distribution.
complaints.mcc_rule4: Campaign silence period begins 48 hours before polling.
